content.full_text:
Fa.rn PorrrIcal Pna,cucns CoumtssloN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(gt6) 322-s660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
Iwre26,2000
Frank Cuny
8048 Mamie Avenue
Oroville, California 95966
RE
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-00-143
Dear Mr. Cuny:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").r Please note that the Commission can only give advice
prospectively and we cannot render any advice relating to past conduct. (Regulation
18239(b)(8)(A).) Therefore, nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any conduct
that has already taken place. 
. ''
QUESTION
Are you required to register as a lobbyist?
CONCLUSION
Based on the information you have provided, we are unable to answer your question
However, we are providing guidelines which will enable you to make that determination.
I Govemment Code section 81000 - 91015. Commission regulations appear attitle2,
sections 18109 - 18996, of the California Code of Regulations.


Page2
File No. I-00-143
FACTS
You are a senior citizen on social security and have been elected president and director of
a non-profit 501(c)(a) organization called Califomia Citizens For Health Freedom Inc. (CCHF).
You are a full time volunteer for this organization and receive no pay for your efforts. However,
you are reimbursed for travel and office supply expenses. About half of your time is spent
contacting the offices of state legislators and state agencies doing legislative advocacy and
educating on issues related to alternative medicine.
CONCLUSION
Section 82039 defines a lobbyist as
"(A)ny individual who is employed or contracts for economic
consideration, other than reimbursement for reasonable travel
expenses, to communicate directly or through his or her agents
with any elective state official, agency official or legislative
offrcial for the purpose of influencing legislative or administrative
action, if a substantial or regular portion of the activities for which
he or she receives consideration is for the purpose of influencing
legislative or administrative action. "
Key to the definition of lobbyist is the receipt or promise of economic consideration to
lobby. Volunteers who lobby do not fall within the definition of lobbyist, and volunteers who
are reimbursed only for reasonable travel expenses are still deemed to have not received any
compensation. Since you indicate that you do receive some compensation for expenses other
than those related to travel, it is possible you may meet the legal definition of lobbyist
Regulation 18239 (copy enclosed) provides two tests for determining whether any
individual is required to register as a lobbyist. One test applies to "in-house" lobbyists - those
who lobby only on behalf of their employer - while the other test applies to "contract" lobbyists
who lobby on behalf of at least one person/entity other than their employer. If you consider
CCHF to be your employer for any other purposes, e.g., for tax purposes, you should use the test
for in-house lobbyist. On the other hand, if you do not consider CCHF to be your employer, you
should use the test for contract lobbyist.
An "in house" lobbyist is an individual who spends one-third or more of his or her
compensated time in a calendar month, engaging in direct communication, other than
administrative testimony, for the purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action.
(Regulation 1 8239(c).)
"Direct communication" is defined as "appearing as a witness before,.talking to (either by


Page 3
File No. I-00-143
telephone or in person), corresponding with, or answering questions or inquiries from any
qualifying offrcial, either personally or through an agent who acts under'one's direct supervision,
control or direction." (Regulation I 8239(d)(3).)
"Administrative testimony" means attempting to influence administrative action by acting
as counsel in, appearing as a witness in, or providing written submissions, including answers to
inquiries, which become part of the record of any regulatory or administrative agency
proceeding, which is conducted as an open public hearing for which public notice is given, or of
which a record is created in a manner which makes possible the creation of a transcript and to
which full public access is provided; or, any regulatory proceeding of the California Public
Utilities Commission. (Regulation I 823 9(dX 1 ).)
"Influencing legislative or administrative action" is defined as "communicating directly or
taking any other action for the principal purpose of supporting, promoting, influencing,
modifying, opposing, delaying, or advancing arry legislative or administrative action."
(Regulation 1 8239(dX4).)
The definition of "contract lobbyist" is an individual who receives or becomes entitled to
receive $2,000 or more in compensation in any calendar month for engaging in direct
communication, other than administrative testimony, with one or more qualiffing officials for
the purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action. (Regulation 18239(b).)
Enclosed is a copy of the Commission's information manual for lobbyists. If I can be of
any additional assistance please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely
?
Luisa Menchaca
Assistance General
By:
Political Reform Consultant
Enclosures

Enclosures
